Upon notification of claim status, such as payment or return, as per the Warranty Settlement Statement or by the DCS daily answerback (Claim Status Report), the claim’s status should be posted to the Warranty Claim Register. • Post the following data from the Warranty Settlement Statement to the Warranty Claim Register upon receipt of the Warranty Settlement Statement: – Payment/Return date – Claim credit amount • Note any discrepancies between the requested input data per the claim submitted over WebDCS and the Warranty Settlement Statement and add remarks, if necessary, for the difference in the Warranty Claim Register. Examples of discrepancies include: Record Retention & Administration Requirements 156 – Errors or differences in the amount credited. – Claims returned for correction should have the date of return posted and the date of the corrected resubmission posted. – If a returned claim is not to be resubmitted, note it in the remarks section. The Warranty Claim Register and the Warranty Settlement Statement must be retained in the dealership accounting office files. If the Warranty Claim Register is automated, a copy must be printed monthly and retained. The Warranty Settlement Statement should be filed chronologically by statement date and month sequence. Hyundai Warranty Claim Registers are available free through the Hyundai Non-Parts Warehouse. The part number is NP449-MV010. An order quantity of one (1) provides ten (10) register sheets. Record Retention & Administration Requirements 157

title: Warranty Settlement Statement (HMA-4301 Report)
raw_content: HMA issues a weekly Warranty Settlement Statement and a PDI Settlement Statement twice a month. These statements detail all the actions taken on each individual warranty, PDI, and transportation claim received and processed since the last Warranty Statement. This information must be reconciled with the Warranty Claim Register. The Warranty Statement lists the claims in Repair Order Number sequence and groups the claims in several sections. Note: You may reprint your Warranty Settlement Statement via Hyundaidealer.com under the Service tab / WebDCS / DCS Reports. The ‘Report ID’ for the Settlement Statement is HMA- 4301. To view recent Settlement Statements, click on ‘Search All Reports’ and enter a date range. The format for any dates listed on this report are YYMMDD (Year, Year, Month, Month, Day, Day), example: 23 01 31. The sections are as follows: Claims Paid This Statement This section lists warranty claims that have been paid since the last warranty statement was issued. These claims are sorted by repair order. • The “Init Submit” field lists the date you initially submitted the warranty claim. • The “Other Approved” field is the sum of SUBLET AMOUNT and TOWING AMOUNT. Section 2: Claims Paid This Statement–Reprocessed/Credited The claims in this section were originally accepted for payment and then either appealed, charged back via DCS or phone, resubmitted and paid within the same statement period. • The “Other Amount” field is the sum of SUBLET AMOUNT and TOWING AMOUNT. • “Trans Type” is defined as follows: ORIG – Original claim total approved for payment. DEBIT – Chargeback of original claim total approved for payment. PAY – Current claim amount approved for payment. Section 3: Claims Paid Prior Statement–Reprocessed/Credited The claims in this section were originally paid on a previous statement, then either appealed, charged back via DCS or phone charged back, resubmitted and paid. • The “Other Amount” field is the sum of SUBLET AMOUNT and TOWING AMOUNT. • TRANS TYPE relates to the TOTAL AMOUNT. The transaction types are as follows: ORIG – This amount is a reference for you and is NOT factored in the total amount on the summary page. The ORIG amount was paid to you on a previous Record Retention & Administration Requirements 158 statement for this claim. It will NOT be charged back from your account–an adjustment is made to it on the current statement. ADJ – This amount is NOT a chargeback – it is used to determine the “Net Adjusted” payment. CRDT – This amount is NOT a credit – it is used to determine the “Net Adjusted” payment. The CRDT amount is the amount paid to you or the corrected, current claim. • NET ADJUSTED is the net difference amount you will be paid from HMA for your corrected claim. The adjusted payment is calculated by subtracting the ADJ from the CRDT amount. This amount will be factored into the Total Amount at the end of the section and the end of the report. • LAST STATEMENT DATE is the date the original claim was paid. • RETURN REASON/S are listed under each claim to explain why the ADJ was made. Section 4: Claims Paid This Statement– Charged Back The claims in this section were originally accepted for payment on this statement, then either charged back at the request of the dealer or by HMA for reason(s) stated under the “Return Reason” field. The “Net Debit” amount for each claim in this section will always offset the original payment amount. • The “Other Amount” field is the sum of SUBLET AMOUNT and TOWING AMOUNT. • TRANS TYPE defines the TOTAL AMOUNT. The transaction types are as follows: ORIG – Total amount originally paid on the claim. DEBIT – Total amount your claim was charged back. • RETURN REASON/S are listed under each claim to explain why the charge back was made. Section 5: Claims Paid Prior Statement– Charged back This section lists claims that were paid to the dealer on a previous statement, then charged back at the request of the dealer or by HMA for the reason(s) stated under the “Return Reason’s” field. • The “Other Amount” field is the sum of SUBLET AMOUNT and TOWING AMOUNT. • TRANS TYPE defines the TOTAL AMOUNT. The transaction types are as follows: ORIG – Total amount originally paid on the claim. DEBIT – Total amount your claim was charged back. • RETURN REASON/S are listed under each claim to explain why the chargeback was made. Section 6: Claims Currently In Suspense Claims are placed in suspense by HMA for a variety of reasons. The claims will only remain in suspense for a maximum of 48 hours, at which time they will either be paid, sent to Prior Approval Center or returned for more information. This section will list claims placed in suspense at the time the Warranty Statement is printed. Record Retention & Administration Requirements 159 Section 7: Claims Currently Pending PA Center Review Claims are placed in pending PA Center review by HMA for a variety of reasons. This section will list claims placed in pending PA Center review at the time the Warranty Settlement Statement is printed. Claims Returned This Statement Claims in this section, were submitted to HMA and returned for correction or more information since the last issuance of the Warranty Statement. The claims should be corrected, re-entered, and resubmitted through DCS for payment. • RETURN REASON/S are listed under each claim to explain why the claim was returned. Claims Previously Returned But Not Resubmitted Claims in this section were submitted to HMA and returned for correction or more information, on a previous Warranty Statement and not resubmitted. This section contains claims returned within a 90 day period prior to the current statement. The claims should be corrected, re- entered, and resubmitted through DCS for payment. • RETURN REASON/S are listed under each claim to explain why the claim was returned. Section 10: Transportation Claims Paid This Statement This section lists transportation claims that have been paid since the last warranty statement. The claims are sorted by repair order. • The “Init Submit” field lists the date you initially submitted the transportation claim. • The “Other Approved” field is the sum of additional expense(s) claimed (see Section 2.2.4). Section 11: Transportation Claims Paid This Statement – Reprocessed/Credited The transportation claims in this section were originally accepted for payment and then either appealed, charged back via DCS, or phone charged back, resubmitted and paid within the same statement period. • The “Other Approved” field is the sum of additional expense(s) claimed (see Section 2.2.4). • “Trans Type” is defined as follows: ORIG – Original claim total approved for payment. DEBIT – Chargeback of original claim total approved for payment. PAY – Current claim amount approved for payment. Section 12: Transportation Claims Paid Prior Statement – Reprocessed/Credited The transportation claims in this section were originally paid on a previous statement, then either appealed, charged back via DCS, or phone charged back, resubmitted and paid. • The “Other Amount” field is the sum of additional expense(s) claimed (see Section 2.2.4). • TRANS TYPE relates to the TOTAL AMOUNT. The transaction types are as follows: Record Retention & Administration Requirements 160 ORIG – This amount is a reference for you and is NOT factored in the total amount on the summary page. The ORIG amount was paid to you on a previous statement for this claim. It will NOT be charged back from your account – an adjustment is made to it on the current statement. ADJ – This amount is NOT a chargeback – it is used to determine the “Net Adjusted” payment. CRDT – This amount is NOT a credit – it is used to determine the “Net Adjusted” payment. The CRDT amount is the amount paid to you on the corrected, current claim. • NET ADJUSTED is the net difference amount you will be paid from HMA for your corrected claim. The adjusted payment is calculated by subtracting the ADJ from the CRDT amount. This amount will be factored into the Total Amount at the end of the section and the end of the report. • LAST STATEMENT DATE is the date the original claim was paid. Section 13 : Transportation Claims Paid This Statement– Charged back The transportation claims in this section were originally accepted for payment on this statement, and have either been charged back at the request of the dealer or by HMA for reason stated under the “Return Reason” field. The “Net Debit” amount for each claim in this section will always be $0.00 as the chargeback amount will always offset the original payment amount. • The “Other Amount” field is the sum of additional expense(s) claimed (see Section 2.2.4). • TRANS TYPE defines the TOTAL AMOUNT. The transaction types are as follows: ORIG – Total amount originally paid on the claim. DEBIT – Total amount your claim was charged back. • RETURN REASON/S are listed under each claim to explain why the chargeback was made. Section 14: Transportation Claims Paid Prior Statement– Charged back This section lists claims that were paid to you on a previous statement, then charged back at the request of the dealer or by HMA for the reason(s) stated under the “Return Reason/s” field. • The “Other Amount” field is the sum of additional expense(s) claimed (see Section 2.2.4). • TRANS TYPE defines the TOTAL AMOUNT. The transaction types are as follows: ORIG – Total amount originally paid on the claim. DEBIT – Total amount your claim was charged back. • RETURN REASON/S are listed under each claim to explain why the chargeback was made. Section 15: Transportation Claims Currently In Suspense Transportation claims are placed in suspense by HMA for a variety of reasons. The claims will only remain in suspense for a maximum of 48 hours, at which time they will either be paid, sent Record Retention & Administration Requirements 161 to Prior Approval Center or returned for more information. This section will list claims placed in suspense at the time the Warranty Statement is printed. Section 16: Transportation Claims Currently Pending PA Center Review Transportation claims are placed in pending PA Center review by HMA for a variety of reasons. This section will list claims placed in pending PA Center review at the time the Warranty Settlement Statement is printed. Section 17: Transportation Claims Returned This Statement Transportation claims in this section were submitted to HMA and returned for correction or more information since the last issuance of the Warranty Statement. The claims should be corrected, re-entered, and resubmitted through DCS for payment. • RETURN REASONS are listed under each claim to explain why the claim was returned. Section 18: Transportation Claims Previously Returned But Not Resubmitted Transportation claims in this section were submitted to HMA and returned for correction or more information on a previous Warranty Statement and not resubmitted. This section contains claims returned within a 90 day period prior to the current statement. The transportation claims should be corrected, re-entered, and resubmitted through DCS for payment. Section 19: Claims With Outstanding Part(s) Not Yet Received This section lists claims with “Outstanding Parts Request Notices”. HMA’s policy is to chargeback warranty claims when a requested replaced part is not received by the Warranty Technical Center (WTC) within 20 days of the “Date Request”. Section 20: Statement Summary The last page of the report is a summary page. The totals shown on the summary page are factored as follows: 1. The total of claims listed in Section 1 of this report. 2. The total of Section 2 + Section 3 of this report. 3. The total of claims in Section 4 + Section 5. 4. The total of claims in Section 6. 5. The total of claims in Section 7. 6. The total of claims in Section 8. 7. The total of claims in Section 9 8. title: Pre-Delivery Inspection Settlement Statement - HMA-4306
raw_content: The Settlement Statement indicates all claims paid, credit adjusted, and chargeback adjusted since the last statement. This statement is sorted by claim number and contains the following information for each claim: • Claim number • Repair order number • Vehicle identification number • Total amount charged back • Total amount claimed • Date approved (payment date) The last page of the PDI Settlement Statement is a summary of the statement totals that includes: • Total amount charged back • Total number charged back • Total amount claimed • Total number credited • Net amount approved • Total number processed Parts Retention Requirements 164 Parts Retention Requirements

title: Parts Retention Requirements
raw_content: Parts replaced under HMA Warranty coverages, HMA Customer Satisfaction Funds (Goodwill Claims), Campaign/Recall, or Foreign Vehicle must be retained for 20 calendar days from the date of claim payment. In cases where claims are returned for additional information or correction, the Dealer must continue to retain these replaced parts until 20 days have elapsed from the date of claim payment. Exception: All 12V AGM batteries replaced under warranty must be tagged and held for 10 days from the date of claim payment. See TSB# 24-EE-009H for more details . Please note that the TSB listed is subject to change; for the most up-to-date version, please visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service Tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB. Note: The claim payment date is indicated as the Acceptance Date on the DCS answerback (Claim Status Report) and on the Warranty Settlement Statement as the “Date Approved”. Core Policy Reman engines and transmission Assemblies will have a core charge that is a separate charge from the dealer net part price. Credit for the core value is issued on the dealership’s monthly parts statement. All core assemblies, except radios and navigation units, are to be returned unless otherwise requested by the National Warranty Technical Center (WTC) as outlined in section 7.2. MOBIS Parts America’s Dedicated Delivery Service carriers (MPA DDS) will arrange for pickup and delivery to the core return center where the core will be inspected, processed, and your credit authorized and electronically transmitted to HMA. Dealers are not responsible for paying freight on these core returns. Cores must be: 1. Fully Assembled 2. With a completed Warranty Parts Tag 3. Completely drained of all fluids (Automatic Transmission, Engine) 4. With a completed Core Return form 5. With a completed Transmission or Engine Diagnosis Worksheet 6. With a copy of the Repair Order (Repair Order must include detailed Condition, Cause and Correction information) or Final Invoice when over-the-counter sale 7. With all plastic/rubber plugs and straps Parts Retention Requirements 165 Go to www.HyundaiDealer.com, click on WebDCS, Parts tab, Core Returns to enter core returns and arrange for core pickup. For proper core and return credit, ALL information entered within the Core Returns portal needs to be accurate and pertaining to the Core being returned. If you have any problems using the website, contact your Hyundai Parts Dealer Support Group at 1-800-545-4515 or MOBIS Parts Portal. Parts Retention Requirements 166

title: Parts Storage Requirements
raw_content: The following procedures are required for all Warranty, Customer Satisfaction (Goodwill), or Campaign/Recall replacement parts retained: • All parts replaced in performance of a Warranty repair, a Customer Satisfaction repair, or Campaign/ Recall are to be given to the Dealer’s Parts Department at such time as the Parts Department issues the replacement part to the technician. • Dealership Parts department personnel must note receipt of the replaced Warranty, Customer Satisfaction, or Campaign/Recall part(s) on the technician’s shop hard copy of the repair order at the time the Dealer’s Parts Department receives the part and issues the replacement part (this function can be performed electronically in a fully computerized system). This notification must be accomplished by a signature or initials on the repair order hard copy by the parts counter person, or by the stamping of such words such as “Parts Received” by use of a stamp on the hard copy of the repair order (digital stamp from DMS is acceptable). • Prior to storage, each part should be externally cleaned and repackaged in the container in which the new replacement part was received. • All parts containing lubricant or fluid must be drained thoroughly and wiped clean. • Cap or plug parts according to instructions in parts bulletin or service bulletin, if applicable. • Dealership Parts Department personnel must then complete a Warranty Parts Tag. The completed tag is to be attached to the replaced part(s). • After the replaced part(s) is tagged, these parts should be temporarily stored in a secured area until it is feasible to transfer them to the Warranty Parts Retention area. This task should be performed at least weekly–more frequently in large volume dealerships. • Dealership Parts Department personnel must place the replaced part(s) in a designated and secured warranty parts storage area (a 10 bin system). Refer to Section 7.4. Note: If the replaced part(s) contain more than one piece, all parts must be kept together within the Warranty Parts storage area in a box, bag (preferably a clear plastic bag), or wired together. • Warranty, Customer Satisfaction, and Campaign/Recall parts must be retained for 20 days from the date of claim payment. Warranty and Customer Satisfaction parts may be requested at any time within this 20 day period by HMA’s Warranty Technical Center, the DPSM, Field Engineer, or other authorized HMA personnel. The DPSM, Field Engineer, or any other authorized HMA staff person may inspect these replaced parts at any time without notification. Therefore, these parts must be available at all times during this 20 day period. Only the DPSM, Field Engineer, authorized Regional personnel, or HMA National Warranty personnel may dispose of these parts prior to the 20 day retention period. If the warranty parts Parts Retention Requirements 167 are not requested or disposed of by HMA personnel within the 20 day retention period, the Dealer is to destroy and scrap the warranty parts after 20 days. Exception: AGM Battery must be retained for 10 days according to TSB# 24-EE-009H . Please note that the TSB listed above is subject to change; for the most up-to-date version, please visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service Tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB. • Warranty, Customer Satisfaction, and Campaign/Recall parts must not be installed on any vehicle, sold at retail, sold for remanufacturing/rebuilding or used in any other application. • In the event that inspection of the warranty parts by HMA reveals that the parts are not defective, not defective as stated in the claim, defective due to improper installation, or removal, or the parts are not available for inspection, a chargeback will be issued. Parts Retention Requirements 168

title: Parts Return Notification
raw_content: Notification of Warranty, Customer Satisfaction (Goodwill), or Campaign/Recall parts to be returned to HMA’s Warranty Technical Center is performed by the following method: • As part of HMA’s Quality Analysis and claim validation program, Dealers will receive from a Warranty Part Return Notice from the HMA National Warranty Technical Center (WTC). These requests for parts will have various return locations. Refer to the destination within the Warranty Part Return Portal (WebDCS) for proper Ship-To address. The Warranty Parts Return Notices and corresponding Parts Return Labels are issued on a daily basis. The Parts Return Labels issued are to be affixed to the back side of the applicable Warranty Parts Tags, and the requested parts with the attached Parts Tags are to be returned to the respective WTC location. Please pay special attention to the notice and label to ensure that parts are grouped and packaged correctly. Dealers will also receive an automated email notification of their “WTC Part Return Status”. Note: Dealers must enter their email address when completing the “Ship from” dealer information on your initial use of the Web Based WTC Part Return System (See Section 7.6) All parts listed on the WTC Parts Return Notice must be returned. The Warranty Settlement Statement, which is issued twice a month, also contains an “outstanding part(s) not yet received” section. This section shows requested parts that have not yet been returned to HMA’s WTC. Occasionally, HMA personnel may inspect, retrieve or dispose of a part prior to the end of the 20 day parts retention period. In such cases, if a parts return notice appears for a part that was previously disposed of by HMA personnel, the Dealer must furnish the WTC with the Warranty Parts Receipt, signed by the DPSM, RWM, FSE, or other authorized individual. The Dealer should retain a photo copy of receipt that has been returned to the WTC. • Replaced Parts or HMA processed signed Warranty Parts Receipt must be received by the WTC within 20 days from issuance of the Warranty Parts Return Notice. If not received, chargebacks will be issued on an automatic basis and will occur approximately

title: days after the issuance of the first request for the replaced part(s).
raw_content: • Parts replaced under warranty coverages, HMA customer satisfaction (Goodwill) funds, or Campaign/ Recall must be retained for 20 days from the date of claim payment approval. In cases where claims are returned for additional clarification, the Dealer must continue to retain these parts until 20 days have elapsed from the date of claim payment. If the parts are not requested by HMA within the 20 day retention period, the Dealer is to destroy and scrap the parts. * Note: AGM Battery must be retained for 10 days according to TSB# 24-EE-009H . WTC must also receive battery within 10 days of the initial request for parts. Please note that Parts Retention Requirements 169 the TSB listed above is subject to change; for the most up-to-date version, please visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service Tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB. Note: Your Warranty Settlement Statements include a Parts Scrap Report (HMA-4330). The Parts Scrap Report includes all parts that were approved over 20 days prior to Settlement Statement date and that now may be scrapped. Parts Retention Requirements 170

title: Warranty Parts Tag
raw_content: The Warranty Parts Tag (P/N NP445-00011) must be completed and attached to the replaced Warranty, Customer Satisfaction, or Campaign/Recall part upon receipt of the replaced parts by the Dealership’s Parts Department personnel from the technician. The following information must be entered on the tag: • Dealer Code (required) • Repair Order Number (required) • Claim Number (optional) • Date of Repair (required) • VIN (required) • Mileage (required) • Part Number (required) • Reason Replaced? (required) By filling out Dealer Code, repair order and part number, the Dealer can quickly locate a part requested by the WTC. Parts Retention Requirements 171

title: The Ten-Bin System
raw_content: HMA requires that the Dealer utilize a 10-Bin Warranty parts retention system. In the 10-Bin system, each of the 10 bins is numbered from zero to nine and corresponds to the last digit of the repair order number. There are 10 separate bins, each of which is large enough to contain the volume of Warranty, Customer Satisfaction, and Campaign/Recall replaced parts required. It is imperative that a Dealer employee (preferably a Dealer Parts Department employee) be given the responsibility for maintenance and security of the Warranty parts retention area. Twenty (20) days after the Dealer is notified of claim payment, the related parts are to be removed from the Warranty parts retention area, destroyed and scrapped, provided these parts are not requested to be returned by HMA. Determination of which parts are eligible for the over twenty (20) day scrap, can be accomplished by using the Parts Scrap Report (HMA-4330) section of your Warranty Settlement Statements or the acceptance date on the DCS claim answerback (claim status report). All 12V AGM batteries replaced under warranty must be tagged and held for 10 days from the date of claim payment. Please refer to TSB# 24-EE-009H* for 10-day AGM battery retention . Regular security and maintenance of the warranty parts retention area assures that only those parts subject to retention, inspection, and possible return are in the Warranty parts retention area. *Please note: The TSB listed above is subject to change; for the most updated version, please visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under Service tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB.
